title: Caruelina parviflora
intro: Caruelina parviflora, also known as Mexican tea or Yerba de la Santa Maria, is a plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Caruelina parviflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to support immune function and alleviate various health complaints.
